shortcuts_help.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts_help.no_agents_note: Note: Most functionality is unavailable until you've created your first agent.
shortcuts_help.customizable_hint: Many shortcuts can be customized from Settings → Shortcuts.

confirm_delete.title: Confirm Deletion
confirm_delete.message: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}?

history_help.title: History Panel Guide
history_help.intro_description: The History panel tracks a synopsis of your work sessions, providing a searchable log of completed tasks with full context preservation.

entry_types.title: Entry Types
entry_types.user_description_before_history: Synopsis entries from your interactive work sessions. Created manually with
entry_types.user_description_since_last: (creates a synopsis of everything since the last
entry_types.auto_description: Entries automatically generated by the Auto Runner after each task completes. These include success/failure indicators and human validation status.

status.title: Status Indicators
status.validate_description_before: You can validate auto tasks from the detail view by toggling the

details.title: Viewing Details
details.description: Click any history entry to open the full details view, which shows:

resume.title: Resuming Sessions
resume.description_before: Each history entry preserves the Claude session ID. Click
resume.description_after: to continue that conversation with all its context intact.
resume.pick_up_description: This lets you pick up exactly where the task left off, make tweaks, or continue building on the work that was done.

time_cost.title: Time & Cost Tracking
time_cost.description: Each entry displays the elapsed time and cost (when available), helping you understand the resource usage of individual tasks.

activity_graph.title: Activity Graph
activity_graph.description: The bar graph in the header visualizes your activity over a configurable time period. Changing the lookback period filters both the graph and the entry list below it—only entries within the selected window are shown.
activity_graph.right_click_description: to choose from multiple lookback periods: 24 hours, 72 hours, 1 week, 2 weeks, 1 month, 6 months, 1 year, or all time.
activity_graph.click_bar_description: to jump to entries within that time bucket.
activity_graph.hover_description: Hover over any bar to see the exact count and time range.

per_session.title: Per-Session Storage
per_session.description_before: History is stored in per-session files with a limit of
per_session.description_after: This provides better isolation and scalability compared to a single global file.
per_session.toggle_description_after: toggle button to switch between viewing only the current session's history or a cross-session view of all history for the project.

cross_session.title: Cross-Session Memory
cross_session.description_before: AI agents are automatically aware of your history files, giving them a form of
cross_session.description_after: This means agents can understand the automatic and manual interactions you've taken across all of your different sessions.
cross_session.json_description: Each session's history is stored as a JSON file that agents can read to understand completed tasks, decisions made, and work patterns—even from other tabs.
cross_session.ssh_note: Cross-session memory is not available for SSH remote sessions, as the history file is stored locally and cannot be accessed by agents running on remote hosts.

intro.description_before: Auto Run is a file-system-based document runner that automates AI-driven task execution. Create markdown documents with checkbox tasks, and let AI agents work through them one by one, each with a fresh context window. Run single documents or chain multiple documents together for complex workflows—a collection of Auto Run documents is called a

setup.title: Setting Up a Runner Docs Folder
setup.description: When you first open the Auto Run tab, you'll be prompted to select a folder containing your task documents. This folder will store all your markdown files with tasks to automate.
setup.change_folder_before: You can change this folder at any time by clicking

format.title: Document Format
format.description_after: in your Runner Docs folder. Each file can contain multiple tasks defined as markdown checkboxes:
format.processing_description_before: Tasks are processed from top to bottom. When an AI agent completes a task, it checks off the box
format.processing_description_after: and exits. The next agent picks up the next unchecked task.

tasks.title: Creating Tasks
tasks.quick_insert_action: to insert a new checkbox at your cursor.
tasks.description: Write clear, specific task descriptions. Each task should be independently completable—the AI starts fresh for each one without context from previous tasks.
tasks.tip_description_after: for easy tracking in history logs.

images.title: Image Attachments
images.description_before: Paste images directly into your documents or click the camera button to attach files. Images are saved to an
images.description_after: subfolder and linked with relative paths.
images.context_description: Use images to provide visual context—screenshots of bugs, UI mockups, diagrams, or reference materials that help the AI understand the task.

single_doc.title: Running a Single Document
single_doc.click_run_after: to configure auto-run. By default, the currently selected document is ready to run.
single_doc.spawn_description: The runner spawns a fresh AI session for each unchecked task. When a task completes, the agent checks it off and exits. If tasks remain, another agent is spawned for the next task.
single_doc.file_path_description: The document is provided to the agent as a file path, giving it direct access to read and modify tasks.

multi_doc.title: Running Multiple Documents
multi_doc.add_docs_after: in the configuration to select additional documents. Documents are processed sequentially in the order shown.
multi_doc.drag_reorder_description: Use the grip handle to rearrange documents in the queue.
multi_doc.skip_description: Documents with zero unchecked tasks are automatically skipped.

variables.title: Template Variables
variables.description: Use template variables in your documents and agent prompts to inject dynamic values at runtime. Variables are replaced with actual values before being sent to the AI.
variables.quick_insert_action: to open an autocomplete dropdown with all available variables.
variables.usage_after: Use them to create reusable templates that adapt to different contexts.

reset.title: Reset on Completion
reset.description_middle: on any document to keep it available for repeated runs. When enabled, Auto Run creates a
reset.original_never_modified: the original document is never modified
reset.timestamps_before: Working copies are named with timestamps (e.g.,
reset.timestamps_after: and serve as an audit log of each loop's work. You can delete them manually when no longer needed.
reset.duplicate_description: Reset-enabled documents can be duplicated in the queue, allowing the same document to run multiple times in a single batch. Since originals are untouched, interruptions leave your source documents pristine.

loop.title: Loop Mode
loop.description_after: to continuously cycle through the document queue until all documents have zero tasks remaining.
loop.perpetual_description: Combined with reset-on-completion, this creates perpetual workflows—perfect for monitoring tasks, recurring maintenance, or continuous integration scenarios.

playbooks.title: Playbooks
playbooks.description_after: is a collection of Auto Run documents configured to run together. Save your batch run configurations for quick reuse. A playbook stores:
playbooks.load_description: Load a saved playbook with one click and modify it as needed—changes can be saved back or discarded.
playbooks.sharing_description_before: Export playbooks as ZIP files to share with others, or import playbooks you've received. Browse the
playbooks.sharing_description_after: to discover and download community-contributed playbooks for common workflows.

history.title: History & Tracking
history.session_pill_before: Click the session ID pill to jump directly to that AI conversation and review what the agent did. Use

readonly.title: Read-Only Mode
readonly.description_before: While Auto Run is active, the AI interpreter operates in
readonly.description_after: You can send messages to analyze code, but file modifications queue until Auto Run completes.
readonly.indicator_after: indicator as a reminder. This prevents conflicts between manual and automated work.
readonly.tip_description: For parallel work without read-only restrictions, create a worktree session from the git branch menu in the session list. Worktree sessions operate in isolated directories, allowing Auto Run and manual work to happen simultaneously.

stopping.title: Stopping Auto Run
stopping.description_after: in the header or Auto Run panel to gracefully stop. The current task completes before stopping—no work is left incomplete.
stopping.resume_description: Completed tasks remain checked. Resume anytime by clicking Run again.

new_instance.directory_warning_acknowledge: I understand the risk and want to proceed
new_instance.directory_warning_recommendation: We recommend using a unique directory for each managed agent.
new_instance.folder_picker_ssh_disabled_tooltip: Folder picker unavailable for SSH remote. Enter the remote path manually.
new_instance.folder_picker_ssh_disabled_tooltip_with_: Folder picker unavailable for SSH remote ({{host}}). Enter the remote path manually.
new_instance.nudge_message_description: {{current}}/{{max}} characters. This text is added to every message you send to the agent (not visible in chat).
new_instance.nudge_message_placeholder: Instructions appended to every message you send...
new_instance.path_is_file_error: Path is a file, not a directory
new_instance.path_not_found_error: Path not found or not accessible
new_instance.provider_change_warning: Changing the provider will clear your session list (tabs). Your history panel data will persist.
new_instance.ssh_unable_to_connect_description: Select a different remote host or switch to Local Execution.
new_instance.working_dir_readonly_description: Directory cannot be changed. Create a new agent for a different directory.

projects.footer_count: {{count}} repositories • Contribute to open source with AI
projects.all_issues_in_progress: All issues are currently being worked on
projects.no_outstanding_work: No outstanding work for this project
projects.select_issue_details: Select an issue to see details
projects.blocked_dependency_message: Blocked by a dependency — the maintainer will unblock when prerequisites are met
projects.will_clone_message: Will clone repo, create draft PR, and run all documents

active.check_pr_tooltip: Check for merged or closed PRs
active.no_contributions_description: Start a contribution from the Projects tab
active.pr_on_first_commit: PR will be created on first commit

history.no_contributions_description: Your contribution history will appear here

help.about_description_part1: Symphony connects Maestro users with open source projects seeking AI-assisted contributions. Browse projects, find issues labeled with
help.about_description_part2: , and contribute by running Auto Run documents that maintainers have prepared.
help.register_project_description: Want to receive Symphony contributions for your open source project? Add your repository to the registry:
help.register_project_tooltip: Register your project for Symphony contributions

preflight.gh_required_description_part2: ) to create draft PRs and manage contributions. It is not currently installed on your system.
preflight.gh_not_auth_description_part2: ) is installed but not authenticated. Symphony needs GitHub access to create draft PRs and manage contributions.
preflight.build_tools_description: Symphony will clone this repository and run Auto Run documents that may compile code, run tests, and make changes. Before proceeding, make sure you have the project's build tools and dependencies installed on your machine (e.g., Node.js, Python, Rust toolchain, etc.).
preflight.build_tools_suggestion: Consider cloning the project first and verifying you can build it successfully. Without the right toolchain, the contribution is likely to fail.

group_chat.description_part1: A Group Chat lets you collaborate with multiple AI agents in a single conversation. The
group_chat.description_part2: manages the conversation flow, deciding when to involve other agents. You can
group_chat.description_part3: any agent defined in Maestro to bring them into the discussion. We're still working on this feature, but right now Claude appears to be the best performing moderator.
group_chat.no_agents_message: No agents available. Please install Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, or Factory Droid.
group_chat.moderator_warning_message: Changing the moderator agent will restart the moderator process. Existing conversation history will be preserved.

leaderboard.join_description_before: Join the global Maestro leaderboard at
leaderboard.join_description_after: . Your cumulative AutoRun time and achievements will be displayed.
leaderboard.invalid_email_error: Please enter a valid email address
leaderboard.email_privacy_description: Your email is kept private and will not be displayed on the leaderboard
leaderboard.click_link_description: Click the link in your email to complete registration. This will update automatically.
leaderboard.resend_confirmation_description: We'll send a new confirmation email to {{email}}. Click the link to get your auth token.
leaderboard.enter_auth_token_description: Copy the token from your confirmation email or the confirmation page.
leaderboard.opt_out_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to remove yourself from the leaderboard? This will request removal of your entry from runmaestro.ai.
leaderboard.auth_token_lost_message: Your email is confirmed but we seem to have lost your auth token. Click "Resend Confirmation" below to receive a new confirmation email with your auth token.
leaderboard.email_confirmed_message: Email confirmed! Your stats have been submitted to the leaderboard.
leaderboard.confirmation_expired_error: Confirmation link expired. Please submit again to receive a new confirmation email.
leaderboard.check_email_message: Please check your email to confirm your registration.
leaderboard.profile_submitted_message: Profile submitted! Stats are synced via Auto Runs. Use "Pull Down" to sync from other devices.
leaderboard.auth_token_recovered_message: Auth token recovered and stats submitted successfully!
leaderboard.profile_updated_message: Your profile has been updated! Use "Pull Down" to sync stats from the server.
leaderboard.submission_failed_check_token_error: Submission failed. Please check your auth token.
leaderboard.confirmation_email_sent_message: Confirmation email sent! Please check your inbox and click the link to get your auth token.
leaderboard.resend_failed_error: Failed to resend confirmation email. Please try again.
leaderboard.synced_updated_message: Synced! Updated to {{serverHours}}h {{serverMinutes}}m from server (was {{localHours}}h {{localMinutes}}m locally)
leaderboard.already_in_sync_message: Already in sync! Local and server stats match.
leaderboard.local_ahead_message: Local is ahead ({{hours}}h {{minutes}}m). No sync needed - your next submission will update the server.
leaderboard.no_server_record_message: No server record found. Submit your first entry to create one!
leaderboard.email_not_confirmed_error: Email not yet confirmed. Please check your inbox for the confirmation email.
leaderboard.invalid_token_error: Invalid auth token. Please re-register to get a new token.
leaderboard.opted_out_message: You have opted out of the leaderboard. Your local stats are preserved.
leaderboard.auth_token_recovered_complete_message: Auth token recovered! Your registration is complete.

autorun_setup.checkbox_tasks_description: Use markdown checkboxes (- [ ]) to define tasks that can be automated

agent_selection.title: Create a Maestro Agent
agent_selection.select_provider: Select the provider that will power your agent.
agent_selection.note_text_before_command: The new agent wizard captures application inputs until complete. For a lighter touch, create a new agent then run
agent_selection.note_text_end: button in the Auto Run panel. The in-tab wizard runs alongside your other work.
agent_selection.ssh_error_hint: Please select a different remote host or switch to Local Machine.
agent_selection.announce_ssh_error: Unable to connect to remote host: {{error}}
agent_selection.announce_detection_complete_auto: Agent detection complete{{context}}. {{available}} of {{total}} agents available. Claude Code automatically selected.
agent_selection.announce_detection_complete: Agent detection complete{{context}}. {{available}} of {{total}} agents available.
agent_selection.announce_detection_failed: Failed to detect available agents. Please try again.

steps.autorun_panel_description: This is the Auto Run panel where your Playbook is being executed right now. Each task from your Phase 1 document is being processed automatically by the AI agent. Watch as checkboxes get marked off! Press {{goToAutoRun}} to jump here anytime.
steps.autorun_panel_description_generic: This is the Auto Run panel. Place markdown documents with task lists here to have the AI execute them automatically. Tasks are checked off as they complete. Press {{goToAutoRun}} to jump here anytime.
steps.document_selector_description: The document selector shows all the Auto Run documents we created together. After the first document completes, you can select the next one and continue building your project.
steps.document_selector_description_generic: The document selector shows all documents in your Auto Run folder. Select different documents to view or run them. You can organize work into phases or any structure you prefer.
steps.file_explorer_description: The Files tab shows your project's file structure. As the AI creates and modifies files, you'll see them appear here. The file tree can be searched. Double click a file to open it in a tab for preview and edit. Right click a file for other options such as opening Markdown documents in a graph view. Press {{goToFiles}} to jump to the file panel from anywhere.
steps.file_explorer_description_generic: The Files tab shows your project's file structure. As the AI creates and modifies files, you'll see them appear here. The file tree can be searched. Double click a file to open it in a tab for preview and edit. Right click a file for other options such as opening Markdown documents in a graph view. Press {{goToFiles}} to jump to the file panel from anywhere.
steps.history_description:
The History tab tracks all AI interactions in your session. Auto Run entries are tracked automatically, and separate from manual interactions. You can toggle history per-message using the "History" bubble (with the clock icon) in the input area. Configure the default value under Settings → General.

Switch between the list view and the details view to drill into any entry. From the details view you can also resume the session where that entry took place.

History also serves as memory for all Maestro agents—they know how to locate and parse the history file, giving them context about prior work. Press {{goToHistory}} to jump here.

steps.main_menu_description: The hamburger menu gives you access to settings, themes, the project wizard, and more. You can also re-run this tour anytime from here under "Introductory Tour".
steps.main_menu_description_generic: The hamburger menu gives you access to settings, themes, the New Agent Wizard, and more. You can re-run this tour anytime from here.
steps.remote_control_description:
The LIVE/OFFLINE indicator controls a built-in web interface for remote control. Toggle it on to generate a local URL and QR code—scan it with your phone to control Maestro from the couch, the kitchen, or anywhere on your network.

If you have Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) installed, one click opens a secure tunnel—no API keys, no login, no configuration. Access Maestro from anywhere, even outside your home network.
steps.remote_control_description_generic:
The LIVE/OFFLINE indicator controls a built-in web interface for remote control. Toggle it on to generate a local URL and QR code—scan it with your phone to control Maestro from anywhere on your network.

If you have Cloudflare Tunnel (cloudflared) installed, one click opens a secure tunnel—no API keys, no login, no configuration. Access Maestro from anywhere, even outside your home network.
steps.agents_groups_description:
The agent list shows all your AI coding agents. Each agent is backed by a provider like Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode. You can run multiple agents simultaneously on different projects and quickly switch between them. A red indicator dot marks unread messages.

Organize agents into groups, and with two or more agents you can start a group chat—even across different providers. Press {{focusSidebar}} to focus the agent list.
steps.agents_groups_description_generic:
The agent list shows all your AI coding agents. Each agent is backed by a provider like Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode. You can run multiple agents simultaneously on different projects and quickly switch between them. A red indicator dot marks unread messages.

Organize agents into groups, and with two or more agents you can start a group chat—even across different providers. Press {{focusSidebar}} to focus the agent list.
steps.ai_terminal_description:
This is the AI Terminal where you communicate with your AI assistant. In "AI" mode (shown now), messages go to the AI. You can also switch to "Terminal" mode for direct shell commands.

Use tabs liberally. Create one for every task, bug, question, or whatever. Each tab is a fresh context. Tabs can be closed and later recalled. There's tooling available on tabs too, such as export, send to another agent, and publish as Gist.

Your favorite browser shortcuts work here: {{newTab}} for new, {{closeTab}} to close, {{reopenClosedTab}} to reopen the last closed tab.

Any prior session you've had with your provider can be recalled as a tab, even if that session occurred with the provider directly.
steps.ai_terminal_description_generic:
This is the AI Terminal where you communicate with your AI assistant. In "AI" mode, messages go to the AI. Switch to "Terminal" mode for direct shell commands.

Use tabs liberally. Create one for every task, bug, question, or whatever. Each tab is a fresh context. Tabs can be closed and later recalled. There's tooling available on tabs too, such as export, send to another agent, and publish as Gist.

Your favorite browser shortcuts work here: {{newTab}} for new, {{closeTab}} to close, {{reopenClosedTab}} to reopen the last closed tab.

Any prior session you've had with your provider can be recalled as a tab, even if that session occurred with the provider directly.
steps.ai_terminal_tab_search: The {{icon}} icon on the left of the tab bar opens a searchable tab overview.
steps.agent_sessions_description: The Agent Sessions button lets you browse previous conversations with your AI agent. Access it via Quick Actions ({{quickAction}}) or the {{agentSessions}} shortcut. Resume past sessions, search through history, and continue where you left off.
steps.agent_sessions_description_generic: The Agent Sessions button lets you browse previous conversations with your AI agent. Access it via Quick Actions ({{quickAction}}) or the {{agentSessions}} shortcut. Resume past sessions, search through history, and continue where you left off.
steps.input_area_description: Type your messages here to communicate with the AI. During Auto Run, this area may be locked while tasks execute. You can queue messages to send after the current task completes. Press {{focusInput}} to quickly jump here.
steps.input_area_description_generic: Type your messages here to communicate with the AI. You can also use slash commands and @ mentions for files. Press {{focusInput}} to quickly jump here.
steps.input_area_icons: Look for these controls: {{penLine}} opens an expanded prompt editor, {{image}} lets you attach files (or just paste), {{history}} toggles session history, {{readOnly}} prevents file changes, {{thinking}} toggles display of agent thinking, and {{enterKey}} switches the submit hotkey.
steps.input_area_defaults_hint: Defaults for these toggles can be changed in Settings → General.
steps.terminal_mode_description: Press {{toggleMode}} to switch between AI mode and Terminal mode. Terminal mode gives you a direct shell for running commands yourself.
steps.terminal_mode_description_generic: Press {{toggleMode}} to switch between AI mode and Terminal mode. Terminal mode gives you a direct shell for running commands yourself.
steps.keyboard_shortcuts_description: Maestro is keyboard-first. Press {{help}} anytime to see all available shortcuts. You're now ready to build amazing things!
steps.keyboard_shortcuts_description_generic: Maestro is keyboard-first. Press {{help}} anytime to see all available shortcuts. You're ready to go!
